chapters.chapter_summary: Patient was positioned supine with shoulder roll elevating the right shoulder and head comfortably turned to the left, exposing the temporal parietal area. General anesthesia was administered.
A circular bone flap was elevated using a single burr hole, exposing the underlying relaxed dura. The dura was relaxed chemically using hypertonic saline and mannitol.

Upon opening the dura, the tumor was easily distinguished from the normal brain. I performed sleep mapping and used sodium fluorescein guided microscopy combined with computer navigation to confirm the location of the tumor. Dissection of the tumor from the surrounding brain tissue was meticulous.

I removed the tumor in one piece in a gross total fashion. Sodium fluorescein guided microscopy showed an area suspicious for residual tumor, which was further explored and removed with no injury to the surrounding brain.

Hydrogen peroxide solution-soaked cotton balls were laid over the cavity of the tumor resection site for both destruction of neoplastic cells and hemostasis. The cavity was covered with a hemostatic agent.
The dura was re-approximated and duroplasty was completed. The bone flap was re-approximated, and the border between the bone flap and the cranium was covered with synthetic bone filler.

Post-operative MRI scans showed no radiographic evidence of residual or recurrent tumor. The pathology of the tumor was glioblastoma multiforme with MGMT promoter methylation and IDH1 or IDH2 mutation not detected.

SOAP.Subjective: I performed a craniotomy and resection of a brain tumor located at the temporoparietal junction in a 20-plus year old patient who presented with headaches, progressive weakness on the left side of the body, and difficulty with gait and balance.
SOAP.Objective: I identified a large mass with significant local mass effect and vasogenic edema. I observed the tumor being easily distinguished from the normal brain upon opening the dura. I used sodium fluorescein guided microscopy and computer navigation to confirm the location of the tumor.
SOAP.Assessment: I assessed the procedure as successful with the tumor removed in one piece. I identified and removed additional suspicious areas for residual tumor. Post-operative MRI scans showed no evidence of residual or recurrent tumor.
SOAP.Plan: I closed the wound in layers. The patient was discharged home with no neurological deficit. The pathology of the tumor was glioblastoma multiforme with MGMT promoter methylation and IDH1 or IDH2 mutation not detected.
